cross_scene_arcs.title: From Architect to Ethical Guardian
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ariadne evolves from a technically skilled dream architect into the moral compass of the team, increasingly focused on the ethical implications of inception and the psychological cost of manipulating reality.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ariadne seeks to prove her architectural capability through technical mastery.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She actively challenges the mission's ethical boundaries and demands accountability for emotional harm.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The tension between moral integrity and mission success remains unresolved, questioning whether noble ends justify psychological manipulation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting Cobb's Subconscious: A Descent into Emotional Truth
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ariadne's relationship with Cobb transforms from student-mentor to one of emotional confrontation and psychological intervention, as she uncovers and challenges the guilt that threatens the mission.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ariadne enters Cobb's dreams out of academic curiosity and professional duty.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She becomes emotionally invested in his redemption, directly confronting his denial and offering moral clarity.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Cobb does not fully accept her support, leaving his emotional healing incomplete and their relationship in a state of fragile hope.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Unraveling of Reality: Understanding Inception's True Cost
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ariadne grapples with the philosophical and psychological conflict between dream and reality, culminating in her active role in defending reality against Mal's illusion in Limbo.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: She begins to understand paradoxical architecture as a technical tool.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She takes decisive action to uphold reality, shooting Mal's projection and forcing Cobb to choose truth over illusion.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The nature of reality remains philosophically ambiguous, and Ariadne's own convictions are implicitly tested by Mal's persuasive rhetoric.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Rise to Leadership in Crisis
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ariadne ascends from a novice participant to a decisive leader during critical moments, asserting authority and guiding the team through escalating dream instability.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: She is overwhelmed by her first shared dream experience and lacks agency.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She commands the team in a life-or-death crisis, making pivotal decisions that save the mission.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her leadership emerges reactively under duress, and her long-term role within the team remains undefined.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Burden of Leadership and Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Arthur consistently assumes the role of operational anchor, striving to maintain control over increasingly unstable dream environments and team dynamics. From stabilizing the initial pitch to Saito, to reestablishing the dream loop, and ultimately confronting system failures and insubordination, his arc traces a trajectory of escalating responsibility and diminishing control.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Arthur establishes himself as the stabilizing force during the team's pitch to Saito, ensuring professionalism and coherence.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Arthur is physically overpowered by Saito and loses command of the extraction, marking the collapse of his authority despite his best efforts.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Arthur can regain operational control in the face of emotional volatility and systemic breakdowns remains uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fracturing Trust with Cobb
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Arthur's relationship with Cobb deteriorates from loyal partnership to principled opposition, driven by Cobb's emotional entanglements and concealment of critical risks. The arc culminates in Arthur's acceptance of Cobb's death, symbolizing the irreversible end of their collaboration.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Arthur confronts Cobb about Mal's presence, asserting professional boundaries while still operating as a trusted ally.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Arthur confirms Cobb's death and shifts focus to evacuation, emotionally detaching in the face of loss.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The long-term emotional toll of losing Cobb—his closest partner—and whether Arthur can reconcile loyalty with ethical dissent remains unprocessed.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Skepticism to Strategic Engagement
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Arthur evolves from a skeptical analyst questioning the feasibility of inception to an active architect of its execution. His arc reflects a shift from intellectual resistance to hands-on leadership in preparing the team and environment for the mission.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Arthur articulates the impossibility of inception, grounding the team in rational skepticism.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Arthur transforms the workshop into a functional base and endorses Eames's plan, fully committing to the mission's success.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Despite his engagement, Arthur's personal doubts about the mission's ethical and psychological costs remain unspoken.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Guardian of Dream Integrity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Arthur emerges as the enforcer of dream-world rules, from totem integrity to architectural stability. He repeatedly confronts threats to procedural discipline, whether from internal team decisions or external subconscious defenses.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Arthur teaches Ariadne about the sacred nature of totems, establishing foundational rules for dream navigation.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Arthur identifies the instability of three-layer dreams, forcing the team to confront a core technical limitation.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether the rules Arthur upholds can survive the mission's escalating chaos and the team's willingness to bend them.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Cost of Mission Failure
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Arthur's status shifts from mission controller to survivor as the operation collapses. Each node marks a step in the erosion of control, culminating in the acknowledgment of death and mission failure.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Arthur awakens from failure, assessing damage and re-engaging with urgency.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Arthur confirms the deaths of Cobb and Saito, transitioning from intervention to evacuation as the last operational leader.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Arthur's future role—whether as a leader, mentor, or retiree—remains undefined in the aftermath of total mission collapse.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Primary Story Trajectory
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Cobb moves from Cobb receives a phone call from his children, which intensifies his emotional struggle between his longing to return home and the reality of his exile, while also revealing his deep guilt and helplessness over his children's belief that he may never come back. to Cobb confronts and rejects Mal's projection, overcoming his guilt and emotional attachment to the idealized memory of her in limbo., with the linked nodes capturing the main cross-scene trajectory.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Cobb's primary goal—returning home to his children—is reaffirmed as the emotional core of his motivation, driving his commitment to complete the dangerous inception job.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Cobb breaks free from the emotional grip of his past, achieving clarity about what is real and solidifying his resolve to return to the real world.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The true faces of Cobb's children remain unseen, leaving a lingering emotional uncertainty about whether he will fully reconnect with them in reality.
